```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    // Create a buffer_adaptor for the input buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);

    // Create a binary_stream using the buffer_adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    // Calculate required space: header (4 bytes) + items (each uint32_t is 4 bytes)
    const size_t required_space = sizeof(header) + items.size() * sizeof(uint32_t);

    // Check available space using buffer_adaptor's free() method
    if (adaptor.free() < required_space) {
        return false;
    }

    // Use the stream's operator<< to write the header and items
    stream << header;
    stream << items;

    // Return true to indicate success
    return true;
}
```

```cpp
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);

    // Check if there's enough data for the header
    if (adaptor.size() < sizeof(PacketHeader)) {
        // The exam doesn't specify error handling. We'll return a default-constructed header.
        return PacketHeader{};
    }

    // Create a binary_stream with the buffer_adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);

    // Use binary_stream's span() method to get a view of the header fields
    auto header_span = stream.span<PacketHeader>(1);

    // Construct and return the PacketHeader from the span view
    return header_span[0];
}
```
